story: Mom vanished five days ago, leaving only her warm dent in the bed. I followed the tug into the pines; March rain had hosed away footprints. Back at the fence line, old Mr. Hargreeve saw me bolt toward the woods and, uneasy, phoned the sheriff to send search-and-rescue. The ground opened into a fifteen-foot hole. At the bottom, Mom was clawing earth, eyes red; she didn't look up when I yelled. I ran for Dad’s ladder; when I came back she was deeper, legs kicking like pale twigs. I grabbed one and got kicked off. Night thickened, the hole keeping its own silence.
implausible_knowledge: Back at the fence line, old Mr. Hargreeve saw me bolt toward the woods and, uneasy, phoned the sheriff to send search-and-rescue.
question: With night closing in and her mother still digging deeper, what will the narrator do next to try to reach her?
answer_with_knowledge: Stay at the rim to keep talking to her mother and wait to flag down the arriving rescuers.
answer_without_knowledge: Climb down into the hole which is very deep after her mother.
